title: Standard Employment Contracts. The Department, through tripartite
content: 54 consultation involving the seafarers and the private sector, shall determine, formulate and 55 establish minimum, separate and distinct SECs for seafarers, in accordance with 56 accepted international standards and maritime practices. These SECs, which shall be 57 reviewed periodically to keep them attuned to international requirements and demands, 58 shall be the minimum requirement in every individual contract approved by the 59 Department. 60 61 40 The Department may also develop ship category, skills, or country-specific SECs to better 1 address peculiarities and needs of seafarers. Likewise, a special contract for cadets may 2 be developed. 3 4 The SEC between the shipowner and the overseas seafarer shall be in writing and shall 5 include, but not be limited to, the following information and terms subject to R.A. No. 6 10173: 7 8 (a) Seafarer’s full name, date of birth, age, birthplace, permanent or residential 9 address; 10 11 (b) SRB Number, Seafarer’s Identity Document Number, Seafarer’s Registration 12 Number, and other necessary documentation as may be required by appropriate 13 government agencies; 14 15 (c) Shipowner’s name and address; 16 17 (d) LMA’s name and address; 18 19 (e) Place and date of execution of the SEC; 20 21 (f) Duration of the contract; 22 23 (g) Point of hire; 24 25 (h) Capacity in which the seafarer is to be employed; 26 27 (i) Amount of the seafarer’s salary, and the formula used for calculating the same; 28 29 (j) Maximum hours of work and minimum hours of rest; 30 31 (k) Wages and wage-related benefits, which include, but are not limited to, overtime 32 pay, holiday pay, vacation leave pay, premium pay, paid leaves, and 13th month 33 pay, if applicable; 34 35 (l) Compensation and benefits for occupational injury, sickness, or death; 36 37 (m)The mandatory process for the determination of the final and binding disability 38 grading to be determined by a DOH-accredited third doctor who is an expert in the 39 disability of the seafarer, in case of conflict between the findings of the company 40 and the seafarer’s doctor; 41 42 (n) Social security and welfare benefits, including compulsory insurance coverage as 43 provided under Section 37-A of R.A. No. 8042, as amended by R.A. No. 10022, 44 and Section 6(n) of R.A. No. 11641; 45 46 (o) Stipulations on repatriation or similar undertakings; 47 48 (p) Separation pay and retirement pay, if applicable; 49 50 (q) Reference to the CBA, or other similar instruments, if applicable; 51 52 (r) Other benefits provided by law, company policy, or CBA; and 53 54 (s) Termination of the contract and the conditions thereof, including: 55 56 (1) If the contract is for an indefinite duration, the conditions entitling either party to 57 terminate it and the period to give notice which shall not be less for the 58 shipowner than for the seafarer; 59 60 (2) If the contract is for a definite duration, the date fixed for its expiry; and 61 62 41 (3) If the contract is for a voyage, the port of destination and the time which has to 1 expire after arrival before the seafarer’s discharge. 2 3 The SEC shall be in English, or translated in the language known to the seafarer, and 4 executed in four (4) original copies before the commencement of the employment. 5 6 The shipowner and the seafarer shall each have a signed original of the agreement. In 7 addition, a signed original shall be made available on board the ship. 8 9 Likewise, the SEC shall be part of the discussion in the PDOS. 10 11
